channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistani Man Testifies About Alleged Iranian Pressure in US Assassination Plot
item.description: A Pakistani national, Asif Merchant, is on trial in the United States for allegedly planning to assassinate former President Donald Trump, along with other US politicians. According to reports, Mercha...

item.title: Tanzania Pushes for Emergency Healthcare Training to Improve Child and Maternal Health
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Tanzania has directed all hospital administrators and local council directors to prioritise investment in emergency healthcare training for medical staff. Dr Florence Samizi,...

item.title: Understanding Context: How Different Perspectives Shape Meaning
item.description: The article explores the complexity and varied uses of the term 'context', highlighting that its meaning is far from straightforward. While appeals to context often seem irrefutable, context itself is...

item.title: WRA Introduces Mobile App for Easier Water Permits and Payments
item.description: Kenya's Water Resources Authority (WRA) has rolled out a mobile application aimed at modernising water management across the country. The WRA Customer Mobile Application enables users to apply for wat...

item.title: David Beckham's Life Celebrated in BBC Documentary Highlighting Iconic Moments
item.description: David Beckham is to be celebrated in a BBC Two documentary titled 'A Life In Ten Pictures', marking thirty years since his famous halfway line goal in 1996. The programme will explore Beckham's life a...

item.title: UK Nuclear Submarine Base Risks and Global Nuclear Arms Escalation
item.description: The article examines growing nuclear threats through the lens of Britain's Devonport dockyard in Plymouth, the UK's primary site for maintaining, refueling, and decommissioning nuclear-powered submari...

item.title: State House Budget Surges to KSh 17 Billion Without Parliamentary Approval
item.description: On Friday, Kenyan newspapers highlighted the massive increase in the State House budget, which has nearly doubled to KSh 16.998 billion this fiscal year. This surge was made possible by an emergency c...

item.title: Labour Council Confirms Interviews, Not Qualifications, Determine Promotions in Gauteng Schools
item.description: The Education Labour Relations Council (ELRC) has dismissed a claim of unfair labour practice by veteran teacher Mpho Grace Senoelo against the Gauteng Department of Education over the appointment of ...

item.title: Indian Seafarers on Oil Tanker Targeted During Escalating US–Iran Conflict in Gulf
item.description: In the latest escalation of the US–Iran conflict, a Bahamas-flagged crude oil tanker named Sonangol Namibe was attacked by an Iranian remote-controlled boat carrying explosives near Iraq’s Khor al Zub...

item.title: Jimmy Lai to Serve 20-Year Sentence After National Security Conviction in Hong Kong
item.description: Hong Kong’s pro-democracy ex-publisher, Jimmy Lai, who founded the now-defunct Apple Daily, has decided not to appeal his conviction under the national security law, according to his legal team. Lai, ...

item.title: Tottenham struggle under Igor Tudor amid looming relegation threat
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur's current season woes continue under interim manager Igor Tudor, who made his stadium debut in a match that ended in a 3-1 defeat to Crystal Palace. The article paints a vivid pictur...

item.title: Bright Fireball Observed Lighting Up Calgary Sky
item.description: Residents in Calgary witnessed a spectacular astronomical event as a bright fireball streaked across the night sky on March 5, 2026. Eyewitnesses reported a vivid trail of light, visible for several s...

item.title: TSN Printing Plant Nears Completion, Set to Boost Efficiency and Revenue
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The construction of the new printing factory at Tanzania Standard Newspapers (TSN) is making good progress. As of March 5, 2026, building works have reached 71.2 percent completion, wh...

item.title: Marcos Reaffirms Support for Skills Development Programs for MSMEs
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. emphasized his administration's ongoing commitment to support reskilling and upskilling initiatives for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) during th...

item.title: Understanding Common Congenital Heart Diseases and Their Development
item.description: The human heart is one of the earliest and most vital organs to develop in an embryo, beginning to beat just four weeks after fertilization to supply nutrients and oxygen necessary for survival. Any d...

item.title: US Coast Guard Conducts Security Assessment at Lagos Ports to Ensure Compliance with ISPS Code
item.description: The United States Coast Guard don start security assessment for selected Nigerian ports, including Tin-Can Island Port, to check if dem dey comply with the International Ship and Port Facility Securit...

item.title: Theatre review: Afterglow explores intimacy but struggles with depth
item.description: The Sydney production of S. Asher Gelman's 2017 play 'Afterglow' attempts to explore polyamory, intimacy, and emotional vulnerability through a physically intense performance. Opening with three naked...

item.title: U.S. and Israel Strikes on Iran Raise Questions About International Law and Stability
item.description: On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched military strikes against Iran, despite ongoing Omani-mediated talks intended to de-escalate tensions. The intervention reflects a recurring ...

item.title: Federal Constitutional Court becomes fully operational after swearing in of additional judges
item.description: Islamabad: The Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) has officially started functioning after two more judges, Justice Rozi Khan Barrech and Justice Arshad Hussain Shah, were sworn in by Chief Justice Am...

item.title: Anthropic Plans Legal Challenge Against DOD Supply-Chain Risk Designation
item.description: Anthropic, the AI company founded by Dario Amodei, has announced plans to challenge the U.S. Department of Defense's (DOD) decision to label it a supply-chain risk. This designation could prevent the ...
